name: Christina Aguilera
description:
'''Christina María Aguilera''' ({{IPAc-en|ˌ|æ|ɡ|ɪ|ˈ|l|ɛər|ə}} {{respell|AG|il|AIR|ə}}, {{IPA|es|kɾisˈtina maˈɾi.a aɣiˈleɾa|lang}}; born December 18, 1980) is an American singer, songwriter, actress, and television personality. An [[Cultural impact of Christina Aguilera|influential figure in music]], Aguilera is noted for her four-[[octave]] [[vocal range]], [[melisma|melismatic]] singing style extending into the use of [[whistle register]], and continual reinvention of [[Public image of Christina Aguilera|her image]] and sound. Referred to as the "[[Honorific nicknames in popular music|Voice of a Generation]]", she was ranked among the [[Rolling Stone's 200 Greatest Singers of All Time|greatest singers of all time]] by ''[[Rolling Stone]]''. Her works have incorporated socially conscious and sexual themes, generating both controversy and critical acclaim.

After appearing on ''[[The Mickey Mouse Club#1989–1994 revival: The All New Mickey Mouse Club|The All New Mickey Mouse Club]]'' (1993–1994), Aguilera recorded the theme song, "[[Reflection (song)|Reflection]]", for the [[animated film]] ''[[Mulan (1998 film)|Mulan]]'' (1998) and signed a record deal with [[RCA Records]]. She rose to fame as a [[teen idol]] with her [[Christina Aguilera (album)|self-titled debut album]] (1999), which topped the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]] and garnered three US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] number-one singles: "[[Genie in a Bottle]]", "[[What a Girl Wants (Christina Aguilera song)|What a Girl Wants]]" and "[[Come on Over Baby (All I Want Is You)]]". Aguilera took on a more provocative image on ''[[Stripped (Christina Aguilera album)|Stripped]]'' (2002), one of the [[List of best-selling albums of the 21st century|best-selling albums of the 21st century]]. After another [[retro style|retro]]-inspired reinvention, she scored her second US number-one album, ''[[Back to Basics (Christina Aguilera album)|Back to Basics]]'' (2006). Throughout these periods, she amassed worldwide hits, such as "[[Lady Marmalade#Moulin Rouge! version|Lady Marmalade]]", "[[Dirrty]]", "[[Beautiful (Christina Aguilera song)|Beautiful]]" and "[[Ain't No Other Man]]".

During the early 2010s, Aguilera featured on the worldwide top ten singles "[[Moves like Jagger]]", "[[Feel This Moment]]" and "[[Say Something (A Great Big World song)|Say Something]]"; the first of these topped the Hot 100, making her the fourth woman in history to [[List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones#Most consecutive decades charting a number-one song|top the chart over three consecutive decades]]. She also starred in the film ''[[Burlesque (2010 American film)|Burlesque]]'' (2010) and contributed to [[Burlesque (soundtrack)|its soundtrack]], earning a [[Golden Globe Award]] [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song|nomination]]. Aguilera remained musically active, having released a total of [[Christina Aguilera discography|nine studio albums]]. Her other ventures included a role in the television series ''[[Nashville (2012 TV series)|Nashville]]'' (2015); roles in the films ''[[The Emoji Movie]]'' (2017) and ''[[Zoe (2018 film)|Zoe]]'' (2018); becoming an ambassador for the [[World Food Programme]] (WFP); co-founding the company [[Playground (company)|Playground]]; and serving as a coach on the [[reality competition]] show ''[[The Voice (American TV series)|The Voice]]'' (2011–2016).

Aguilera is one of the [[best-selling music artists of all time]], with over 100 million records sold worldwide. She has been regarded as one of the most influential Latin artists in the United States, having helped shape the "[[Latin American music in the United States#2000s Latin pop boom|Latin explosion]]" in the early 2000s with ''[[Mi Reflejo]]'' (2000), the best-selling Latin pop album of that year. ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' named her one of the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]] in 2013. Aguilera's [[List of awards and nominations received by Christina Aguilera|accolades]] include five [[Grammy Awards]], two [[Latin Grammy Awards]], two [[MTV Video Music Awards]], a [[Juno Award]], a [[Billboard Music Awards|''Billboard'' Music Award]], three [[Billboard Latin Music Awards|''Billboard'' Latin Music Awards]], and a star on the [[Hollywood Walk of Fame]]. She was also named a [[Disney Legend]], in recognition of her contributions to [[the Walt Disney Company]].
